title: Combining customs and court remedies against counterfeit goods
p1:
A counterfeit product crossing a border does not always have to reach the market before action can begin.

Many businesses miss a powerful first line of defence: stopping fake goods at customs.
p2:
A common misconception is that court action is the only way to fight counterfeits. In reality, customs authorities can help block suspected fake shipments before they enter the country.

The surprising part? Brand owners can often combine border enforcement with court remedies to create stronger protection.
p3:
Customs action helps prevent counterfeit goods from moving further, while court remedies can address larger issues like injunctions, damages, and stopping repeat offenders.

The practical lesson: businesses should not wait until counterfeit products damage their reputation. A coordinated strategy using both customs measures and legal action can protect customers, revenue, and brand value more effectively.
conclusion: Have you seen businesses rely only on court action while overlooking customs enforcement as a tool against counterfeits?
